idea.hook: Imagine being kicked out of your home... forever!
facts: Our Milky Way has billions of stars, orbiting the galactic center at about 220 kilometers per second.
But some stars get too close to the supermassive black hole, Sagittarius A* (Sgr A*).
Sgr A*'s immense gravity slingshots these 'hypervelocity stars' to speeds over 1,200 km/s (2.7 million mph)!
idea.payoff: These stars are moving too fast to stay in the galaxy, condemned to wander intergalactic space alone.
idea.title: Galactic Exiles! 🚀🌌

scenes.text: Kicked out of your home... FOREVER?
scenes.visual: A visually dynamic animation of a single star being dramatically ejected from a galaxy's spiral arm.

scenes.text: Our Milky Way: Billions of stars, orbiting at ~220 km/s.
scenes.visual: An artistic representation of the Milky Way galaxy, showing many stars orbiting a central point. Subtle motion lines indicate orbital paths.

scenes.text: But some approach the supermassive black hole: Sgr A*.
scenes.visual: Zoom into the galactic center, revealing a glowing, dark sphere representing Sagittarius A* (Sgr A*). A single star appears to be drawn towards it.

scenes.text: Sgr A* slingshots them >1200 km/s (2.7M mph)!
scenes.visual: The star is dramatically slingshotted away from Sgr A* at incredible speed, leaving a bright trail. Speed numbers flash on screen.

scenes.text: Too fast to return. These 'Hypervelocity Stars' wander intergalactic space... alone.
scenes.visual: The ejected star rapidly recedes into the vast, dark emptiness of intergalactic space, the galaxy shrinking behind it until it's just a lonely speck.
